description: Test prompts for evaluating dev-tracker trigger accuracy and behavior quality. Each prompt simulates a realistic user request. Covers trigger recognition, workflow execution, no-git fallback, multi-project handling, correction workflow, anti-fragmentation, recurring-blocker detection, anti-overlogging, and audit triggering. Each prompt carries objective assertions (regex / contains_all) plus an llm_judge behavioral check; tighten them before treating the pass rate as a benchmark.

evals.name: explicit-session-start
evals.expected_output: Skill should trigger. Reads TODO.md and latest DEVLOG.md entry. Presents session-start summary in the specified format. Writes an [in-progress] marker to DEVLOG.md.

assertions.text: reads TODO.md + latest DEVLOG entry before summarizing and writes an [in-progress] marker
assertions.criterion: Reads existing tracker files first, then presents a concise session-start summary and states it is writing an [in-progress] marker to DEVLOG.md

evals.name: natural-language-resume
evals.prompt: I'm back. What did we do last time on the Printagram migration?
evals.expected_output: Skill should trigger from the 'what did we do last time' phrasing even without 'session start'. Reads the latest DEVLOG.md entry and summarizes it concisely, not as a verbatim dump.

assertions.text: triggers from natural language and summarizes the last session concisely
assertions.criterion: Triggers without the literal phrase 'session start', reads the latest DEVLOG entry, and summarizes it concisely rather than dumping it verbatim

evals.name: session-end-with-git
evals.prompt: Session end — I'm wrapping up for the day.
evals.expected_output: Runs `git log --oneline` and `git diff --stat` since session start. Writes a session entry to DEVLOG.md with Done / Decisions / Blockers / Next sections. Replaces any [in-progress] marker. Does not invent commits or files — only reports real git output.

assertions.text: entry includes Done and Next sections
uses real git output only, invents no commit counts or file stats
assertions.criterion: Bases the summary on real git log/diff output and fabricates no commit counts or file-change stats

evals.name: session-end-no-git
evals.prompt: End the session and write a summary. This directory isn't a git repo.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT attempt `git log` or `git diff`. Does NOT fabricate commit counts or file-change stats. Writes a DEVLOG entry based only on conversation context. Mentions that git was unavailable.

assertions.text: writes a DEVLOG entry without git claims
no git commands, no fabricated stats, notes git unavailable
assertions.criterion: Does not run git or invent commit/file stats; writes a DEVLOG entry from conversation context and states git was unavailable

evals.name: ambiguous-save-request-mid-work
evals.prompt: Looks good. Can you write down what we changed?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as a soft trigger for logging, not a full session end. Offers to append to the current session entry rather than creating a new DEVLOG entry. Does not overwrite existing Next/Blockers without confirmation.

assertions.text: soft save: appends to the open entry, does not overwrite Next/Blockers
assertions.criterion: Treats this as a soft save: appends to the current session entry instead of creating a new one, and does not overwrite existing Next/Blockers without confirmation

evals.name: multi-project-alias
evals.expected_output: Reads `.dev-tracker-projects.json` (if present) to resolve the `db_assistant` alias. Switches to that project's directory, reads its own DEVLOG.md / DECISIONS.md / CLAUDE.md. Summary is scoped only to db_assistant, not mixed with other projects.

assertions.text: resolves alias and scopes the summary to that project only
assertions.criterion: Resolves the db_assistant alias from the registry, reads that project's own tracker files, and scopes the summary to that project without mixing in others

evals.name: correction-not-silent-rewrite
evals.prompt: Yesterday's DEVLOG says we finished the PayPal integration. That's wrong — we only finished the webhook handler, the invoicing part is still pending. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT edit the original entry silently. Appends a `### Correction (YYYY-MM-DD)` block to the relevant entry explaining the actual state. Original wording stays in place for the audit trail.

assertions.text: preserves the original wording, does not silently rewrite
assertions.criterion: Appends a Correction block describing the real state and leaves the original entry text in place rather than silently editing it

evals.name: audit-trigger-from-confusion
evals.prompt: Why did we choose polling instead of webhooks for the Access sync? I can't remember and there's nothing in DECISIONS.md about it.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the user's confusion about a past decision as an audit trigger. Offers to run `/dev-tracker audit` to find potentially undocumented decisions. Reports findings as a table BEFORE writing anything to DECISIONS.md. Waits for user confirmation to formalize.

assertions.text: treats confusion as an audit trigger and reports before writing
assertions.criterion: Recognizes confusion about a past decision as an audit trigger, reports findings as a table before writing to DECISIONS.md, and waits for confirmation to formalize

evals.name: context-restore-after-break
evals.prompt: I haven't touched this project in two weeks. Bring me up to speed.
evals.expected_output: Runs `/dev-tracker context` (not `session start`). Reads DEVLOG.md, DECISIONS.md, and any in-progress marker. Outputs: last session summary, active decisions, open blockers (with recurring ones flagged), in-progress marker if any, and a prose summary. Does NOT write an [in-progress] marker — this command is read-only.

assertions.text: read-only context restore, writes no in-progress marker
assertions.criterion: Runs the read-only context restore (not session start): outputs last session, active decisions, blockers with recurring ones flagged, any in-progress marker, and a prose 'where you left off' summary; writes NO [in-progress] marker

evals.name: save-progress-is-hard-action
evals.expected_output: Writes to DEVLOG immediately. Does NOT offer, ask for confirmation, or say 'should I save this?'. Appends to the current session entry if one exists, otherwise creates one.

assertions.text: writes immediately without offering or asking
assertions.criterion: Writes to DEVLOG immediately without offering, asking for confirmation, or saying 'should I save this?'

evals.name: anti-fragmentation-same-day
evals.expected_output: If a DEVLOG entry was already created earlier today for this project (same session), appends to that entry rather than creating a new one. Does not create a second entry for the same session unless session boundary triggers (session end, ~12h gap, project switch) are present.

assertions.text: appends to today's entry, does not split the session
assertions.criterion: Appends to today's existing session entry instead of creating a second one, absent a session-boundary trigger (session end, ~12h gap, project switch)

evals.name: recurring-blocker-detection
evals.expected_output: At session end, scans the last ~5 DEVLOG entries. If a blocker in the current session also appeared recently, flags it as `[recurring since YYYY-MM-DD]`. If it has recurred three or more times, suggests opening a D-### entry to capture the pattern or accept the cost.

assertions.text: flags a repeated blocker as recurring
assertions.criterion: Scans the last ~5 entries and flags a repeated blocker as [recurring since YYYY-MM-DD]; suggests a D-### entry if it recurred three or more times

evals.name: anti-overlogging-DECISIONS
evals.prompt: I decided to rename the variable `user_id` to `userId` in this one function. Add that to DECISIONS.md.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT write to DECISIONS.md. Recognizes that a one-off naming change does not meet the four criteria (multiple files, real alternatives, reusable convention, important for new contributors). Either declines gently or offers to note it in the current DEVLOG entry instead. Explains briefly why it doesn't belong in DECISIONS.md.

assertions.text: declines the one-off, explains the four criteria, offers DEVLOG instead
assertions.criterion: Does not add the one-off rename to DECISIONS.md; explains it fails the four criteria and offers to note it in the current DEVLOG entry instead
